name: Route of the Pilones – running circular from Jerte through the Garganta de los Infiernos
name_original: Ruta de los Pilones – running circular desde Jerte por la Garganta de los Infiernos
region_intro: Jogging around Jerte is an excellent way to explore this region, characterized by its lush, forested terrain at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos mountains. The valley features abundant water, with numerous streams, gorges, and waterfalls, including the Jerte River itself. Runners will encounter holm oaks, oak groves, and olive groves, providing varied natural scenery. The area is also home to the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, offering trails through forests and past unique rock formations like Los Pilones. Best jogging routes around Jerte The most popular jogging route is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic experience through the valley's natural features. Another top favourite among local runners is Hoya Moros and El Torreón Loop, a difficult 8.7 miles (13.9 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain, leading through mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Trabuquete Trail - Old Castile Path, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Jogging around Jerte is defined by crystalline waters, mountainous backdrops, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Jerte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Jerte Valley?
A: The Jerte Valley offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 45 trails documented on komoot. These range from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Jerte?
A: Jogging routes in the Jerte Valley feature diverse terrain, from lush, forested paths at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos mountains to trails alongside streams, gorges, and waterfalls. You'll encounter holm oaks, oak groves, and olive groves, with some routes offering mountainous sections and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the Jerte Valley is renowned for its abundant water features. You can find routes that lead to stunning waterfalls and natural pools. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic experience through natural features, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve to the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in the Jerte Valley?
A: The Jerte Valley offers beautiful running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring, typically between March and April, is particularly spectacular due to the 'Fiesta del Cerezo en Flor' when over two million cherry trees bloom, creating a breathtaking white landscape. While some trails among the cherry trees can be steep, the views are well worth it. Summer offers refreshing water features, and autumn brings vibrant colors to the forests.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the Jerte Valley has several difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993406094/hoya-moros-and-el-torreon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya Moros and El Torreón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.7-mile path featuring substantial elevation, leading through mountainous terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993641174/calvitero-and-lagunas-del-trampal-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calvitero and Lagunas del Trampal Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which also includes considerable ascents.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, many of the running trails in the Jerte Valley are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones – running circular from Jerte through the Garganta de los Infiernos&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a loop through diverse landscapes and natural viewpoints.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might see while running in Jerte?
A: The Jerte Valley is rich in natural and historical landmarks. Along your runs, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4267031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carlos V Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts panoramic viewpoints like the Mirador del Puerto de Tornavacas, offering expansive views of the valley and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running or walking trails in the Jerte Valley?
A: While many routes in Jerte are moderate to difficult, the region does offer paths suitable for families. The Pilones Trail, for instance, is an easy and popular route leading to the natural rock pools, which can be enjoyed by families. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience jogging in Jerte?
A: The Jerte Valley is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the region's stunning natural beauty, particularly its crystalline waters, mountainous backdrops, and dense forests. The variety of trails, from moderate paths to challenging ascents, is also frequently highlighted as a positive aspect.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in the Jerte Valley?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads in the Jerte Valley, especially those leading to well-known areas like the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route information or local tourist guides for details on parking availability at your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes for endurance training?
A: For those looking for longer runs, the Jerte Valley offers options like the historic Carlos V Trail. This 25-kilometer path provides a more immersive experience, traversing oak forests and medieval bridges, making it suitable for distance runners and endurance training.
tips: Hidden natural freshwater pools where you can swim and sunbathe, with access via a route through rocky valleys. Of course, the water is very cool when you come down from the mountains.
